headline: Why Microsoft (MSFT) Stock Is Up Today
summary: Shares of technology giant Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) jumped 5.1% in the afternoon session after the technology sector rallied, pushing the Nasdaq near all-time highs, as investors cheered a potential de-escalation of geopolitical tensions in the Middle East amid a flurry of positive news in the artificial intelligence space.

headline: Tapestry Adds Pinterest CTO To Board To Sharpen Digital Retail Focus
summary: Tapestry (NYSE:TPR) has appointed Matt Madrigal, Chief Technology Officer of Pinterest, to its Board of Directors. Madrigal brings experience in technology and e-commerce from roles at Pinterest, Google, and eBay. Tapestry, the owner of lifestyle brands such as Coach, Kate Spade, and Stuart Weitzman, continues to focus on digital engagement and younger consumers. The addition of Madrigal reflects the growing importance of online discovery, social commerce, and data-driven marketing for...

headline: Musk Asks Suppliers to Move at ‘Light Speed’ on New Chipmaking Plan
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Elon Musk’s lieutenants have reached out to chip industry suppliers including Applied Materials Inc., Tokyo Electron Ltd. and Lam Research Corp. for his envisioned Terafab, early steps in an audacious and likely arduous attempt to break into the production of cutting-edge chips. Staff working for the joint venture between Tesla Inc. and SpaceX have sought price quotes and delivery times for an array of chipmaking gear, people familiar with the matter said. headline: Customer Demand for Continuous Packet Capture Drives Upsurge in Endace Partner Program
summary: AUCKLAND, New Zealand & AUSTIN, Texas, April 16, 2026--Packet capture authority, Endace, has announced a raft of new partners and updated integrations as part of its Fusion Partner Program, including Cisco XDR, Splunk SIEM, Splunk SOAR, Vectra AI, Microsoft Sentinel, Google SecOps, Exabeam LogRhythm, Palo Alto Networks XSOAR, Sumo Logic, and Fortinet FortiNDR.

headline: Google should allow third-party search engines access to data, EU says
summary: The European Commission has sent ‌preliminary findings to Google on ‌proposed measures to comply with the ​EU's Digital Markets Act, which would allow third-party search engines to access Google search ‌data, including ⁠that of artificial intelligence chatbots with search functionalities, ⁠the commission said on Thursday.  Google, the world's most popular search engine, was charged in ‌March 2025 with ​breaching the Digital ​Markets ​Act.

Google, the world's most popular search engine, was charged in ‌March 2025 with ​breaching the Digital ​Markets ​Act.
